summary: Do not ship as written: the plan smuggles an untracked status-precedence flip into a single-flip bugfix. Simpler alternative: none safer than the service-layer ownership predicate; admin-only middleware would violate the creator/payer contract.

findings.title: Single-Flip Contract misses the required 409-to-403 precedence change
findings.body: The plan requires authz to run before finalized-trip and version-CAS checks so unauthorized callers cannot observe lock/version state, but the Single-Flip Contract only declares the currently-successful unauthorized PATCH/DELETE path changing 200 to 403. From the current source, finalized mutations fail 409 before update/delete, and stale versions are mapped to 409; moving authz before those checks also changes unauthorized finalized/stale requests from 409 to 403. The regression test only covers an open, fresh version=0 request, and the preserved contract only keeps 409 for authorized actors, so a competent implementer can either violate the single-flip invariant or leave this security ordering unproved.
findings.recommendation: Reconcile the plan before implementation: either expand the Single-Flip Contract/flips[] to name the unauthorized status-precedence behavior under the same authz root cause and add RED/acceptance cases for outsider finalized-trip and stale-version PATCH/DELETE returning 403, or remove/defer the info-leak ordering requirement so existing 409 behavior remains preserved.

next_steps: Open question: decide whether hiding finalized/version state from unauthorized joined members is part of this bugfix or a separate authorization-hardening flip.
